interests.text: Although a large bonfire is lit in Seurasaari, the celebration is low key as the tradition is to celebrate "the nightless night" at summer cottages in the countryside. Although some celebrate Juhannus in Helsinki as well, the streets are often eerily empty and the doors of the shops closed, making it the most quiet time of year in Helsinki.

interests.title: Tuska Open Air
interests.text: An annual, 3-day heavy metal festival, featuring acts from all over the world, held in July.

interests.title: Helsinki City Marathon
interests.text: The name says it all. Not as famous as the one in New York, but with over 6,000 participants the largest marathon race in Finland.

interests.title: Flow Festival
interests.text: Is a music and arts festival in early August at Suvilahti. Noted for its high-end arrangements marrying music to design and gourmet food and drink, Flow has expanded to include installations, arts and workshops in the past few years. The music presented at Flow is a strong and varied selection of up and coming and established artists from indie-rock to soul and jazz and from folk to contemporary club sounds, both from the Finnish and the international scene.

interests.title: Finland-Sweden athletics competition
interests.text: A yearly athletics international competition held between these two neighboring countries since 1925 – the only one still existing of this kind of two-country competition. The two-day event, held in alternate years in Finland or in Sweden, attracts significant audiences.

interests.title: Helsinki Festival
interests.text: Multi-week annual arts festival in the latter half of August. The peak of the festival is Taiteiden Yö, "Night of the Arts" called "little ''vappu''" by many as the streets are full of revelers. The official event is marked by performing arts through the night. The Night of the Arts was originally organized by local bookstores in the 1990s. It's now organized by the city. During the last few years, the event has slightly returned to its origin as an arts and culture event.

interests.title: Helsinki International Film Festival
interests.text: Also known as ''Rakkautta & Anarkiaa'' (''Love & Anarchy'') and held annually in September, HIFF features a wide selection of films from all over the world. Asian films have been a special focus in the history of the festival that celebrated its 25th anniversary in 2012.

interests.title: Lautapelaamaan
interests.text: Finland's biggest board game event, hosted by the Finnish Board Game Society. Held on a weeked in early November. A whole hall at the Helsinki Cable Factory is available to play board games for the entire weekend, from Friday evening to Sunday evening (the event closes for the nights though). Hundreds of board games available provided by the organisers (note that to loan a game to play, you must leave something valuable as a pawn token, such as your phone or wallet). You can also bring your own board games to play. Suitable for people of all ages, both adults and children.

interests.title: Liquorice and salmiak festival
interests.text: Finns are known for their taste for liquorice flavoured with the salt ammonium chloride, popularly known as salmiak or ''salmiakki'' in Finnish, even if many foreigners may find the taste strange and even repulsive at first. Wanha Satama in Katajanokka holds an entire festival for salty liquorice candy on a weekend in middle November. More liquorice candy than you can shake a stick at, both with salmiak and without. Several vendors offer their candy for sale and give you free samples.

interests.title: Joulu
interests.text: In the weeks before Christmas, Aleksanterinkatu is festively lit up, the Christmas lights of the street are ceremonially lit on the last Sunday in November. The open-air Christmas market formerly held in the Esplanadi Park is nowadays held at the Senaatintori (Senate Square). The Stockmann department store sets up a Christmas themed exhibition with mechanic dolls and animals in their windows at the corner of Keskuskatu and Aleksanterinkatu. Christmas itself is a family event, so on the 24th, everything shuts down and stays closed until December 26.

interests.title: New Year's Eve
interests.text: Like in many other major cities, thousands of Helsinkians gather at Senaatintori next to the Cathedral to welcome the New Year. The event is shown on live television and there is a free outdoor concert as well.

interests.title: Söderskär Lighthouse
interests.text: An old secluded lighthouse island out at sea, in the middle of a bird reserve. Day trips are arranged by [http://www.royalline.fi/articles/1221/ Royal Line], including lunch, a guided tour of the lighthouse (Finnish/English) and a couple of hours to linger on the island, but it is also possible to stay the night.

interests.title: Skippered Day Sailing
interests.text: Visit the coastal archipelago on a 35 ft sailboat, for two hours or full day trips with an experienced skipper. Island hopping is also possible.

interests.title: United Community Church
interests.text: International, bible-based and nondenominational church that welcomes both Finns and foreigners to attend. Services in Helsinki and Espoo on Sundays. Free.

destinations.name: Reykjavik
destinations.insight: Out of scale on every axis. A trip rather than a weekend.

destinations.name: Prague
destinations.insight: The reflexively good answer. Closest, cheapest, lightest.

interests.title: Charles Bridge
interests.text: Connects Old Town with Lesser Town. Its construction started in the 14th century and it is one of Prague's most beautiful structures. During the day, it is a bustling place of trade and entertainment, as musicians busk and artists sell their paintings and jewelry.

interests.title: Czech National Gallery
interests.text: Its most important collections are in the Sternberg Palace (up to the Baroque), St George Convent (Czech Baroque and Mannerism) and Veletržní Palace. The first two are located near and in the castle respectively. Do not confuse them with the Castle Picture Gallery (see above) which is worth visiting on its own right.

interests.title: Veletržní Palace
interests.text: 19th century and modern art.

interests.title: Czech National Museum
interests.text: An association of various museums. The main building is at the Wenceslas Square and is dedicated to natural history. Other branches include museums of the Czech composers Dvořák and Smetana, Czech Music Museum, Historical Pharmacy Museum, Prince Lobkovicz' Collection at the Prague Castle, Czech Ethnographical Museum and Naprstek Anthropological Museum.

interests.title: Prague City Gallery
interests.text: A museum of modern Czech arts divided between several sites most of which are in the old town. Its main building is the House of the Golden Ring at the Old Town Square featuring 20th century Czech art in a beautiful medieval edifice. 19th century Czech art is exhibited at the Troja Castle.

interests.title: Jewish Museum
interests.text: This covers six separate places (four synagogues, the Old Jewish Cemetery and the Memorial Hall) but does not include the Old-New Synagogue, although entrance tickets can either include or exclude the last named. The Old-New Synagogue is expensive in relation to the museum but in view of its age, it's worth including it. The Memorial Hall is particularly moving with exhibits of the writings of children in death camps.

interests.title: Prague City Museum
interests.text: Has several branches throughout town. An absolute must-see for the incredibly detailed cardboard model of nineteenth century Prague by Anton Langweil. The detail is amazing, even down to the colour of the doorways and the design of the windowsills.

interests.title: Prague Card
interests.text: Available for 2, 3 or 4 days. Includes free public transport, airport transfer and a city tour. Free entry to Prague Castle - St.Vitus Cathedral, Royal Palace, Golden Lane, St.George's Basilic; Free entry to Jewish Museum - synagogues and famous Old Jewish Cemetery (6 sites). In total free entry to 50 attractions and discounts on over 30 attractions. Free guidebook with information about the attractions in 7 languages

interests.title: Welcome Card TVCzechia
interests.text: Grants admission to all the Prague Castle short tour, which normally costs 250 Kč. Many of the town's museums and galleries—including all branches of the National Gallery and the National Museum—are also included, and over four days you can easily see 3 times the card's value. As such, this is an excellent choice if you're planning on visiting a lot of museums. The only major attraction that is not included is the Old New Synagogue and Jewish Museum.

interests.title: Prague City Pass
interests.text: Free entry to various attractions in Prague within a 30 days period, various 25% discounts, sightseeing tours. Prague Castle – Old Royal Palace with Vladislav Hall, St. George‘s Basilica, Golden Lane with Daliborka Tower, St. Vitus Cathedral. The ticket is valid for 2 days from first entry. Jewish Museum in Prague – Maisel Synagogue, Spanish Synagogue, Pinkas Synagogue, Old Jewish Cemetery, Klausen Synagogue, Ceremonial Hall. The ticket is valid for 7 days from first entry.

interests.title: National Gallery Gift Ticket
interests.text: If you are an art lover and you are staying in Prague for a longer time, a '''dárková vstupenka''' (gift ticket) for National Gallery may save you money. The ticket is valid for a year and is valid in all exhibitions (both permanent and non-permanent) of National Gallery. Number of visits is not limited. A gift ticket for one person costs 650 Kč, for two persons 1000 Kč. For 240 Kč you can have one-person ticket valid for two days in all "Old Art" exhibitions of National Gallery (Šternberk Palace, Schwarzenberg Palace, St. Anežka Convent), basic entry for these three galleries bought separately would cost you.

interests.title: Cryptex Praha
interests.text: Cryptex Praha is one of the most challenging escape games in Prague. If you want to play, you have to make a reservation in advance on their website and show up on time to play. They offer free game-play for the teams who make it without help and cheating.

interests.title: Getaway Prague The real-life escape game
interests.text: Getaway Prague is located in the center, 100m from Namesti Miru metro station. The game requires good observation skills, logical thinking and good communication among the players. Booking in advance is required and can be made online or via phone.

interests.title: Lostrooms
interests.text: Biggest escape games network in Prague, operating 9 different adventures with unique scenarios. Rooms are available at 3 offices: Žitomirska 38, Mečislavova 18, Korunní 56, Opatovická 18.

interests.title: Prague Spring
interests.text: The most famous classical music festival in Czech republic

interests.title: Cruise Prague
interests.text: Offers a wide range of regular and private cruises.

interests.title: JazzBoat
interests.text: Combines cruising and jazz concerts.

interests.title: Prague Flights
interests.text: Offers sightseeing flights by airplane, helicopter or in hot air balloon and tandem skydiving.

interests.title: Treasure Hunt Prague
interests.text: Treasure Hunt Prague is a dynamic team competition, that makes use of modern technology and GPS-enabled tablets and lets you visit a wide range of Prague's historical monuments in a record time. Suitable for larger groups and companies.

interests.title: We Bike Prague
interests.text: With We Bike Prague team you can easily discover the city of Prague and the area around. Very good quality bike to rent for you self guided trip in the city and multidays trip trough the countryside. We Bike Prague is also specialized in long distance bike trip. Good bikes, panniers and maps can be rented for your cycling holiday in the Czech Republic.

interests.title: BIKO Adventures Prague
interests.text: BIKO offers mountain bike, road bike and outdoor activities off the beaten track in Prague and in the Czech Republic. From easy to advanced. High end bike rental: touring bikes, hardtail and full suspension MTB, road bikes, e-MTB.

interests.title: Prague tours center
interests.text: A free offer for the visitors of Prague: The Prague Tours Centre is offering free connection and charge up of your electric bikes and Segway PT. While connected, the devices are guarded by a supervisor and, in the meantime, you can take walk around Prague, visit a museum or your preferred restaurant. In the Prague Tours Centre the tourists have a possibility of depositing their bikes together with baggage which they do not want to carry or leave in the streets. The Prague Tours Centre also offers facilities for washing your bike or borrowing a complete bike repair kit for free.

interests.title: Running Tours Prague
interests.text: Activity for those into '''running''' who want to explore the city and stay fit. It shows runners of all abilities around the city's musts while on the run. A traveling runner introduces the best of the Prague to their running shoes and feels just like a local runner. It usually takes 50–120 mins and 7–13km. Your running in Prague is 100 percent customizable as to date, time, pace and distance.

destinations.name: Warsaw
destinations.insight: Increasingly confident. Excellent value, quiet emissions.

interests.title: The Cross-Culture Warsaw Festival
interests.text: World music festival. Concerts and music workshops, documentary screenings, exhibitions.

interests.title: Jewish Culture Festival - The Singer's Warsaw
interests.text: August.

interests.title: Warsaw Film Festival
interests.text: October.

interests.title: Planete+ Doc Film Festival
interests.text: Documentary films. May.

interests.title: Burn Selector Festival
interests.text: Electronic & Alternative Music Festival. September.

interests.title: Orange Warsaw Festival
interests.text: Big pop music festival on the national stadium. May.

interests.title: Warsaw Summer Jazz Days
interests.text: July.

interests.title: Warsaw Autumn
interests.text: International Festival of Contemporary Music. September.

interests.title: Long Night of Museums
interests.text: A great opportunity to wander around with your date or friends and grab an ice cream cone from one of the many cafes that stay open late.  Most museums and galleries will stay open past midnight. Noc Muzeów usually occurs around mid-May.

interests.title: Legia Warszawa
interests.text: Football team from Warsaw.

interests.title: Polonia Warszawa
interests.text: Football team from Warsaw.

interests.title: Warsaw Eagles
interests.text: An American football team in Warsaw.

interests.title: Atelier Amaro
interests.text: Author's cuisine restaurant of chef Wojciech Modest Amaro. The restaurant does not offer ''à la carte'' dishes, in fact it doesn't have a fixed menu at all. Smart casual dress code is expected.

interests.title: Senses
interests.text: A "private dining" restaurant, author's cuisine of chef Andrea Camastra. Reservation required.

interests.title: Targ Śniadaniowy
interests.text: Organised every weekend in various parts of town, usually

destinations.name: Krakow
destinations.insight: Old-town gravitas at modern-budget pricing. A frequent favourite.

destinations.name: Budapest
destinations.insight: Thermal city. Excellent emission-to-experience ratio.

interests.title: Hungaria Koncert
interests.text: Operates cruises with lunch or dinner daily at 14:00, 19:00 and 20:00. This service is 90 minutes with hot buffet lunch or dinner. During the cruise, the Parliament, Chain Bridge, Royal Castle, Palace of Arts etc. can be seen.

interests.title: Budapest Spring Festival
interests.text: A dazzling variety of cultural events mainly revolving around classical music and performing arts - including folklore.

interests.title: Formula One
interests.text: Car racing.

interests.title: Firework above Danube River
interests.text: National Holiday.  Sound & Light Show with firework. Around half hour open air program.

interests.title: Jewish Summer Festival
interests.text: Another array of cultural and music events, with a Jewish touch.

interests.title: Sziget
interests.text: Festival on Óbudai Sziget (Óbuda Island) An institution attracting rock fans, world music hippies and the usual festival crowd every year in August. It has become one of the best-known festivals in Europe, offering a multitude of cultural, culinary and musical events. Day tickets cost €45 and festival passes, including camping privileges cost €170 if purchased before April 15 and €200 thereafter. Festival passes without camping privileges cost €30 less. Sleeping in a tent under the open sky instead of a hotel room gives the complete festival feeling. (Safe boxes are available for valuable personal belongings).

interests.title: Wasabi
interests.text: Excellent Japanese and Korean food. Lunch 3790 HUF. The Buda unit: Szépvölgyi ut 15 (train station Szépvölgyi ut) 11AM-11PM.

interests.title: Trófea Grill
interests.text: The best among all-you-can-eat (buffet) and all the alcohol you can drink. Best to book a table in advance. Has 4 locations. 1 on Buda downtown by Margaret Bridge (Margit körút 2)

interests.title: Leroy Cafe
interests.text: (See district article Pest for details) Mid to high-priced restaurant chain that offers Hungarian classics with other Italian and European cuisine.  Very fashionable interiors and popular with the well-paid white collar crowd. Reservations are recommended during traditional peak times. When eating here, always make sure that you won't be slapped on an extra 100% service charge. Read the menu before entering the restaurant carefully and insist on talking to someone who speaks English.

interests.title: Govinda
interests.text: Great vegan/vegetarian restaurant in Budapest. You can choose from different menus every day or just order separate dishes;  moderately priced. The Govinda chain has three restaurants in Budapest. The main restaurant is at Vigyázó Ferenc utca 4, ''Govinda Vega Corner'' is at Papnövelde utca 1, and the ''Govinda-Buda'' is at Árpád fejedelem utca 33. [http://osm.org/go/0KfA3sYkc--?m See maps],  [http://osm.org/go/0KfA3sYkc--?m Vigyázó Ferenc utca 4]

interests.title: Edeni Vegan
interests.text: Cafeteria style restaurant, large portions, relatively cheap. Food may vary depending on day, time, and dishes chosen. Tofu goulash recommended. Staff is very helpful at explaining the dishes so ask what they are if you don't know. Note: CASH ONLY. There is an ATM at the bank across the street.

destinations.name: Bratislava
destinations.insight: A discreet detour. Best paired with Vienna or Budapest.

interests.title: Bratislava Castle
interests.text: The castle itself is already opened after reconstruction and hosts the exhibitions. There is also a restaurant at the castle open till after the actual castle tours into the night.

interests.title: Slovak National Gallery (SNG)
interests.text: For a taste of '''visual arts''', pay a visit to the National Gallery at the embankment between Starý most and Nový most with permanent collections of Slovak and European medieval art pieces, although the temporary exhibitions tend to be far more interesting.

interests.title: Bratislava City Gallery (GMB)
interests.text: Bratislava City Gallery is also a good pick to see displays of historical fine arts, paintings and sculptures along with interesting temporary exhibitions.

interests.title: Danubiana Art Museum
interests.text: If you like modern art better, pay a visit to the Danubiana Art Museum on the southern edge of Bratislava but be aware that it is too far for a stroll, with little to no public transport connections, and is best reached by car or taxi.

interests.title: Milan Dobes Museum
interests.text: This small museum features modernist Op-art. It lies in the city center and is recommended to all interested in the development of modern art.

interests.title: National Museum
interests.text: An unique exhibition on ancient Egypt is being held here until end of August 2008, together with permanent archeological collections and more,

interests.title: Natural Science Museum
interests.text: Echoing the communist era, the natural science exhibitions have an interesting collection of artifacts and is slowly being transformed into a modern era exhibition,

interests.title: Gerulata
interests.text: This is an ancient Roman military camp with archeological findings on display. If you're into all things Roman, you should also consider a visit to the nearby '''[http://www.carnuntum.at/de Carnuntum]''' which has a large archeological site and is only a few minutes by car from Bratislava.

interests.title: Museum of Transport
interests.text: With a display of historical vehicles, situated in the first railway station in the city, very close to the current Main Railway Station,

interests.title: Museum of Trade
interests.text: This museum has some remarking pieces of historic advertising plates and other artifacts.

interests.title: Jewish Community Museum
interests.text: Rare Jewish ritual tools, gold-embroidered mantles protecting the holy biblical scrolls, unique photographs of students who had attended the famed Bratislava rabbi school that reveal even to this day the atrocities of the Holocaust. It is situated in the Heydukova Street Synagogue, which is the only synagogue in Bratislava. Constructed in 1923-1926, it is a Cubist building designed by the Bratislava-based Jewish architect Artur Szalatnai-Slatinský and is listed as a national cultural monument. Orthodox in orientation, the synagogue still serves as an active Jewish house of worship. The Jewish Community Museum with a permanent exhibition “The Jews of Bratislava and Their Heritage” is installed upstairs and during the summer season is open to the public up till September, every Friday 1 till 4PM and every Sunday 10AM-1PM.

interests.title: Museum of City History
interests.text: Including the museums of history and music as well as the city dungeon and the exhibition on medieval justice.

interests.title: St. Martin's Cathedral
interests.text: The largest and one of the oldest churches in Bratislava, situated below Bratislava Castle. The Gothic cathedral, formerly the coronation-church of several Hungarian kings, begun in 1204, and reconsecrated in 1445, was restored in 1861-80. The tower is surmounted by a pyramid bearing a gilded Hungarian royal crown.

interests.title: St. Clare's Church
interests.text: A Gothic church on Klariská street, in the historical center; currently used as a concert hall.

interests.title: Church of St. Elisabeth
interests.text: Nicknamed the '''Blue Church''' (Modrý kostolík), this is a beautiful Jugendstil church finished in 1913.

interests.title: Primate's Palace
interests.text: Currently the seat of the Mayor of Bratislava.

interests.title: Old Town Hall
interests.text: The Old Town Hall is open to the public as a museum.

interests.title: Grassalkovich Palace and gardens
interests.text: Or the '''Presidential Palace''' (Prezidentský palác) - a Rococo/late Baroque summer palace with a French garden, used as a seat of the President of Slovakia. In one of the garden's alleys you will see a row of trees planted by famous people such as Juan Carlos I (King of Spain). In front of the Palace you will see the Slovak National Guard.

interests.title: Academia Istropolitana
interests.text: The oldest historic University in the area, which is now occupied by the state of Slovakia, from the 13th century.

interests.title: Slovak National Theatre
interests.text: The historic building of the Slovak National Theatre, built in 1886.

interests.title: Slovak Radio Building
interests.text: Its main building is a peculiar 60m high reversed pyramid from the communist era and a landmark in sharp contrast with the building of the Slovak National Bank just across the street.

interests.title: Slavín monument
interests.text: On the top of the hill behind the castle, overlooking the entire city. This is a monument in memory of Soviet casualties in the liberation battle of Bratislava in World War II. It is the highest place in the city, and thus the best place for viewing the city. Slavin actually is a cemetery and thus rather quiet. On warm nights it's a very romantic place, allowing you to sit in the shadows of the monument and look at the traffic below. To get there, take trolleybus no. 203 from Hodžovo námestie (in front of the Presidential Palace) in the direction Búdková and get off in 9 minutes at the last stop, then walk 500 m along Stará vinárska and then Pažického streets. Slavin is near an embassy district.

interests.title: Roland Fountain
interests.text: Built by stone cutter Andreas Luttringer and commissioned by Hungarian king Maximilian in 1527, this was the first fountain in Bratislava.

interests.title: Michael's Gate with Tower
interests.text: This 51m high tower above the gate with its green copper roof is one of the best known and the oldest buildings in Bratislava. It was built in the 14th century as one of the four gates to the city.

interests.title: Jewish Cemetery, Jewish Community Museum and Bratislava Synagogue
interests.text: The only synagogue in Bratislava.

interests.title: SNP Bridge
interests.text: A bridge over the Danube river, with its flying saucer-shaped structure housing a restaurant called [http://bratislava-slovakia.eu/places/sightseeing/ufo-tower "UFO"]. There is an observation deck on its roof, open from 10:00-23:00 daily, offering great views of the old town, as well as the apartment blocks in Petržalka.

interests.title: Speedboats.sk
interests.text: Enjoy the beautiful view of Bratislava and the surrounding nature of the river Danube during a Bratislava boat trip.

interests.title: Prasna basta
interests.text: The best old Pressburger restaurant in town. Just few steps from Michalska street (turn left directly after Michalska tower). Really authentic frequented by locals. With hidden inner garden.

interests.title: Bakchus Vinaren
interests.text: A must place for every Bratislava visitor. The best local and Indian dishes in town, purely Slovak wines and historical setting of a traditional wine cellar.

interests.title: BeAbout
interests.text: Music club near the riverside popular with younger crowd. Good selection of beers including Belgian specialty beers.

interests.title: Camouflage
interests.text: Try this place for cocktails and enjoy the modern ambience.

interests.title: Casa del Havana
interests.text: A Cuban-style restaurant and bar near the centre of the Bratislava Old Town. Not very spacious, but has a comfortable terrace. Particularly famous for the mojito, which has been considered the best in Bratislava, if not in all Slovakia. Mojito 6.49 €.

interests.title: Grandes Melones
interests.text: Great for cocktails. Friendly staff and air conditioning. Don't be put off by the name, you could spend days in this place.

interests.title: UFO
interests.text: If you want to go for extraordinary, visit UFO. It looks like a cliché flying saucer and it's a high on a New Bridge (the member of great-towers.com), and you have to use a lift to go there. There is a good restaurant and lounge bar and observation deck. The view is incomparable.

destinations.name: Ljubljana
destinations.insight: Small, green, articulate. A quiet recommendation that keeps surfacing.

interests.title: Triple Bridge (Tromostovje)
interests.text: Tromostovje consists of three separate picturesque bridges located next to one another. According to design by known Slovenian architect Jože Plečnik, the two bridges on the sides were built in the 1930s; the middle bridge is originally from 1842 and has been only redesigned at that time. The neighbouring Prešeren square with the statue of Slovenian greatest poet France Prešeren is the central location of downtown Ljubljana and a common meeting point. From here, cross Ljubljanica and turn left for Open Market and the Dragon Bridge, or go straight and then right for the old town.

interests.title: Old Town
interests.text: Squeezed between the castle hill and Ljubljanica river is the old town with two squares, Mestni trg (City square) with the Robba fountain and the city hall behind it, and, farther on, Gornji trg (Upper square). Well preserved medieval buildings now house local designer shops, and several popular cafes and restaurants. Although they may look creepy, the perfectly safe narrow lanes lead to charming little squares and buildings.

interests.title: Zmajski Most (Dragon Bridge)
interests.text: Completed in 1901, designed by Croatian Jurij Zainovich. It is guarded by four detailed dragon statues from the city's coat-of-arms. Look out for the dragon motif throughout the city. Be careful around the Dragon Bridge area, as it is on a major busy road just outside the pedestrian zone and near misses (and worse) between inattentive tourists and traffic are common. The dragon bridge is located at the end of the Ljubljana Open Market, just a block or two down the river (north-east direction) from the Triple bridge.

interests.title: Ljubljana Castle
interests.text: The tower has magnificent views all over the city. You can also see the Sava River and Kamnik Alps in the distance. Included is a 3D Movie of the history of Ljubljana from a pre-historic settlement to Roman Empire to modern times (€6.50 for adults, €3.00 for students, seniors).

interests.title: Square of the Republic
interests.text: Where crowds gathered as Slovenia announced its independence from the Yugoslav Federation in 1991. The square has significant importance for Slovene history, as it was a place of important public gatherings (and demonstrations) in the past. Across the road is the Slovene Parliament. Its façade is decorated with artistic nude statues of Slovenes at work and leisure. The square also houses the International Business Centre.

interests.title: Roman Ruins
interests.text: A short walk west of the centre of town are the remains of the Roman City Walls, including a number of pillars from an entrance gate.

interests.title: Krakovo
interests.text: A village-like part of the city connecting the centre to the Trnovo suburb.

interests.title: Metelkova City
interests.text: A self-declared autonomous culture place to gather for alternative artists, many subcultures and youth. What used to be a military barracks is now full of underground artists, bars and nightclubs. Metelkova can get crowded on Fridays and Saturdays. It is within 5 minute of walk from main Train Station, and it is home to a renowned hostel Celica.

interests.title: Tivoli Park
interests.text: The main city park. You can sit down on a park bench and enjoy the sun. Rožnik (the hill overlooking Ljubljana from the west) is a short hike away through the woods. This is a popular weekend destination for the locals.

interests.title: Ljubljana Zoo
interests.text: one of the most beautiful Zoos of Europe

interests.title: University Botanic gardens Ljubljana
interests.text: Ljubljana Botanic Garden is the oldest Slovene cultural, scientific and educational institution. It was founded in 1810, the time of the Illyrian Provinces, as a garden of native flora and a section of the Central School (École Centrale). In the garden is also tropical glasshouse.

interests.title: Architecture Museum of Ljubljana
interests.text: Hosts a permanent exhibition of Jože Plečnik's works.

interests.title: Architecture Museum of Ljubljana: Plečnik Collection
interests.text: An annex of the Architecture Museum devoted to great Slovenian architect Jože Plečnik, who lived in this building from 1921 to 1957. The house contains rooms with the artist’s valuable original furniture and personal objects, an archive of his sketches and plans and a collection of clay, plaster and wooden models, a library, Plečnik’s correspondence, a photo archive, thesis projects of Plečnik’s students and an archive of the Academy of Architecture Collection. Access only by guided tour of not more than 7 people, booked at least five days in advance.

interests.title: Museum of Modern Art
interests.text: For those who prefer contemporary art, houses the national collection of 20th century Slovene art (paintings, sculptures, prints and drawings as well as photography, video and electronic media collections), a collection of works from the former Yugoslavia, and the international collection Arteast 2000+. It is being currently restored.

interests.title: National Gallery
interests.text: Definitely worth visiting if you care about art. It exhibits two important permanent collections that include works of some of the best and most renowned Slovenian (impressionist) painters (Jama, Grohar, Jakopic, Azbe). Since September 2008 you can also see there a restored original of the Robba Fountain, a monument of national cultural heritage.

interests.title: National Museum of Slovenia - Prešernova
interests.text: The oldest and the largest Slovene museum. It was founded in 1821. Existent museum building on the Museum street, was built in 1888 and was the first, building, assigned solely to culture in Slovenia. Today its stores a rich collections of valuable objects. The oldest spring back to the Stone Age, but there are also the newer ones, which are still used in our everyday life. Important columns of museum activity are also rich museum library and unit for preservation and restoring.

interests.title: National Museum of Slovenia - Metelkova
interests.text: In the new building of the National museum of Slovenia on the Metelkova Street, are exhibited collections of the applied art heritage of Slovenia. The permanent exhibition brings together objects of applied arts from the 14th century to the present day.

interests.title: National Museum of Contemporary History
interests.text: Slovenia's 20th-century history museum gives you a real sense of the country's roller-coaster ride through regime after regime. Its immersive exhibits include a walk-through WWI trench. The last exhibit shows the events around the Declaration of Independence from Yugoslavia and the Ten Day War with the Yugoslav Federal Army. All exhibits are translated to English and some to German too.

interests.title: Slovenian Museum of Natural History
interests.text: Features an almost complete skeleton of a 20,000 year old mammoth found near Kamnik in 1938, a 200 million year old fossil fish skeleton found in Triglav National Park, and a new, 13m skeleton of a fin whale. Also has a large mineral collection.

interests.title: Tivoli Gallery - International centre of Graphic Art
interests.text: Specialised in art shows, often in collaboration with international institutions. .

interests.title: Hot Air Balloon Flights
interests.text: Accepts bookings via email or phone, at least a day before the flight. Hot air balloon flights are ONLY early in the morning, starting at Sunrise. Whole programme is about 3-4 h (flight time approx. 1 h).

interests.title: Water City of Atlantis
interests.text: A water park offering 14 pools, thermal baths, slides and saunas. Special section is dedicated for children.

interests.title: Laguna Fun & Spa
interests.text: Offers open-air pools during the day and themed parties at night.

interests.title: Cankarjev Dom
interests.text: Enormous arts and convention centre with concerts and performances

interests.title: Kinoteka
interests.text: Ljubljana's charming film museum where they have at least two movies a day. The movies range from classics to cult movies, from trash and horror to artistic masterpieces. All movies are presented in the original audio language, and equipped with Slovenian subtitles, sometimes also English. On Saturday mornings they have special projections for children. There are also some special events, round tables, premiers etc. all the time. It's a must visit for every film lover. It has also a nice little cafe with small prices

interests.title: Kinodvor
interests.text: This is a small and very cool cinema that shows mostly new movies that are not playing in the blockbuster movie complexes. It has also a lot of special events, a great cafe and movies for children (Sundays).

interests.title: Arena Play & Party
interests.text: A great place to spend an evening. Main features include bowling, pool, laser games and a state-of-the-art 3D XpanD cinema theatre (Movies from €4.59 to €7.93).

interests.title: Kolosej Cinema
interests.text: Ljubljana's main cinema multiplex. Unlike in many other countries, all movies are presented in the original audio language (typically English), and equipped with Slovenian subtitles. Only some showings of computer-rendered and animated movies are dubbed for the children, which is noted.

interests.title: Hot' Horse
interests.text: Huge burgers and other dishes, focused around horse meat. Made to your liking. Don't be fooled by the pictures on the official website, the burgers are huge, larger than at McDonald's or Burger King. Note that you must use an automatic order machine to place your order and pay for it before picking it up from the counter.

interests.title: Falafel
interests.text: This Middle-Eastern dish, together with other meat and vegetables dishes is served in the small fast-food restaurant next to the Dragon bridge.

interests.title: Nobel Burek
interests.text: The place where young people go to get food late in the evening or at night. Different kinds of burek (a leafy dough pie, traditionally with mince meat or cheese filling) available. Arguably the best burek in town.

interests.title: Odprta Kuhna (Open Kitchen) food market
interests.text: On Fridays, during the summer part of the year, the market is buzzing, even in the evening, as locals and tourists alike try the varieties of dishes at the various food stalls of the so called "odprta kuhna" (open kitchen).

interests.title: Cafe Romeo
interests.text: Fusion between cocktail bar and Mexican restaurant. Besides tourists, this place is also popular among the locals.

interests.title: Cantina Mexicana
interests.text: Mexican restaurant set in a small yard together with another restaurant and a pub.

interests.title: Da-bu-da
interests.text: Thai food and cocktail bar, great atmosphere.

interests.title: Emonska Klet
interests.text: Former Ursuline convent. Cellar restaurant serving pizza, salads, and Slovenian food. Live music every night.

interests.title: Figovec
interests.text: Serves pony steaks, horse goulash, and traditional Slovenian food.

interests.title: Foculus
interests.text: Flamboyantly decorated pizzeria and salad restaurant.

interests.title: Gostilna Pod Rožnikom
interests.text: Specialises in grilled dishes from southeastern Europe.

interests.title: Gostilna Sokol
interests.text: This restaurant deals up authentic Slovenian cuisine, complete with all waiters and waitresses dressed up in traditional costume. The food is hearty and served in generous gut-busting portions and very good value for money. The fish cooked in a paper bag with vegetables is highly recommended. There is plenty of meat, fish and vegetarian dishes to choose from. The only downside was the house red, which is very sharp and quite cold, but this is a minor quibble.

interests.title: Joe Pena's
interests.text: Popular Mexican restaurant.

interests.title: Julija
interests.text: Fare of Italian pasta and risotto dishes. Not quite a nice as Luka’s but still a good filling meal, and at a reasonable price.

interests.title: Jurman
interests.text: Affordable prices and very diverse choices. They have themed rooms, such as castle room and fishing room. Children can play in a designated indoor and outdoor playgrounds. The staff is friendly and the service is fast; it usually never takes longer than 10 minutes for 2 or 3 people. You are advised to make a reservation in advance.

interests.title: Luka Gourmet Lunch Cafe
interests.text: One of a line of cafes on Stari Trg with outside seating. Serves up tasty, mainly pasta dishes with reasonable prices and friendly service. Steak, €19.

interests.title: Ljubljanski Dvor
interests.text: Offers 100 different kinds of pizza. Beautiful patio overlooking the river.

interests.title: Pri Škofu
interests.text: This restaurant is heavily promoted by tourist guides, but delivers an inconsistent experience (i.e. can be very good or simple adequate). Their biggest shortcoming is the lack of wine choice. House specialties are gnocchi, black risotto, tenderloin medallions, or grilled aubergines.

interests.title: Šestica
interests.text: Serves hearty meat dishes such as sausages, horse fillets, and steak as well as pasta and risotto.

interests.title: Špajza
interests.text: This restaurant is much larger than you first expect when you initially enter. You walk through several candlelit rooms before you reach the outside terrace. The waiters are initially quite attentive, serving you a nice little aperitif to get you started. The food is a mixture of Slovenian and European, and the menu is kept to a handful of choices for each course. Because of the service, surroundings and food this felt like an expensive restaurant, although by Western standards, it was quite reasonable.

interests.title: Stara Mačka
interests.text: This is one of the best deals in town if you are looking for an intimate steakhouse

interests.title: Zhong Hua
interests.text: Decent Chinese restaurant near the Dragon bridge. The family-run place even has Beijing duck on their menu.

interests.title: Zlata Ribica
interests.text: Great fish restaurant with outdoor seating facing the river.

interests.title: Gostilna Pod Vrbo
interests.text: Local restaurant serving a huge range of Slovenian food - appetisers, starters, main courses, sweets, salads, meat, fish, etc. Friendly staff speak Slovenian, English and Italian, and the menus are indeed in Slovenian, English and Italian.

interests.title: As
interests.text: Considered by many as Ljubljana's fanciest and most expensive, focusing on seafood and an extensive wine choice

interests.title: Cubo
interests.text: Modern International fare.

interests.title: JB
interests.text: Modern International fare, can get expensive by Slovenian standards.

interests.title: Krpan
interests.text: specializing mainly in seafood with an amazing grill experience. Unfortunately there have been some reports of inflated bills.

interests.title: Manna
interests.text: excellent traditional dishes. It advertises "Slow food", but in effect the service is both helpful and fast. The restaurant has friendly and artistic atmosphere.

interests.title: Pri Vitezu
interests.text: High-end restaurant serving swordfish, scallops, snails, duck, and horse fillets.

interests.title: Govinda's
interests.text: Vegetarian restaurant with Indian cuisine and vegan options

interests.title: Cafe Antico
interests.text: Great ambiance - bare wooden floors, high tables and stools, and vaulted ceiling.

interests.title: Cafe Gaudi
interests.text: Nice interior and wide selection of coffees.

interests.title: Čajna Hiša
interests.text: This is a quirky little coffee and tea room, offering many variations on those warm beverages as well as basic breakfasts and lunches. The background music is impeccably cool, and the atmosphere is ideal for some elegant loafing. Linked to the café is a teashop selling drinking paraphernalia and loads of different fruity flavoured teas by the 100g.

interests.title: Geonavtik
interests.text: Nautically themed cafe/bar with travel map and bookshop. Mellow atmosphere.

interests.title: Juice Bar Babo
interests.text: A juice and smoothie bar in the old city center with over 50 combinations of freshly prepared beverages to choose from. Laid-back atmosphere, interesting combination of urban and health freak culture. Good place to meet tourists and interesting locals of all ages.

interests.title: Le Petite Cafe
interests.text: Very popular corner cafe.

interests.title: Maček
interests.text: The most popular cafe along the river.

interests.title: Samsara
interests.text: Summer-only outdoor cafe offers large selection of ice cream & gelato.

interests.title: Sax Pub
interests.text: Really cool colorful spray-painted cafe/bar along the canal.

interests.title: Zvezda
interests.text: Cafe with great ices and pastries.

interests.title: BI-KO-FE
interests.text: A lively artsy little bar that plays excellent jazz music (CD, not live). There is outside seating, but inside is where it seems to be happening. It looks like the place the youngish locals hangout to drink the night away. Coffee, teas, and alcoholic beverages.

interests.title: Breg
interests.text: Classy wine bar along the river.

interests.title: Cutty Sark
interests.text: Lively pub with raucous atmosphere. Usually has live music.

interests.title: Lepa Žoga
interests.text: Main sports cafe/bar with sports regalia along the wall and a good, mixed crowd on most nights. Shows international rugby, all sorts of football games (European, English, Italian and other leagues) and other live sports. Has the best TV coverage in the city, ranging from Sky Sports to local TV stations.

interests.title: Od Žmavca sosed pa ud brata prjatu
interests.text: Markets to the "creative urban type."

interests.title: Patrick's Irish Bar
interests.text: The ubiquitous Irish Pub with Guinness and local beers on tap, with a typical menu of hearty meals available. Also typical is the welcoming atmosphere that seems common to all Irish Pubs. Big Screen TVs show sporting events, (mainly traditionally Irish Gaelic Games and occasionally soccer) but bar staff are happy to change a television to show other sports (Rugby Union, Rugby League, Cricket, AFL, NFL etc.) on request. Watch out for expat and tour groups at popular sporting events from their 'home' country.

interests.title: Pr'skelet
interests.text: Skeleton-themed basement bar in a side alley. Famous for an extensive variety of cocktails, and you get two for price of one, prices vary from €6 to 13.

interests.title: Pod Skalco
interests.text: Popular student pub with an almost 150 year long tradition, frequented almost exclusively by locals. Loud music.

interests.title: Ragamuffin
interests.text: Intimate, reggae cafe-bar with good tunes.

interests.title: Vinoteka Movia
interests.text: Anyone with any interest in wine should visit here. The cosy, candlelit wine bar comes with knowledgeable barmen who can recommend a wine based on your tastes. The wine glasses are huge, so it's hard to tell if they were being stingy or if it has simply spread out. Be careful about asking for ‘samples’, as they will charge you full price. The prices can vary from modest to a remortgage.

interests.title: Zlata Ladjica
interests.text: Fun, energetic pub. Serves mulled wine in winter.

interests.title: Scratch Rock Bar
interests.text: Dedicated to classic, mainstream and underground rock music, offers a wide choice of beers, sandwiches and snacks, and, above all, a varied programme of events related to rock music. The most frequently played styles include classic rock, melodic hard rock, album-oriented rock, progressive rock and blues rock. Metal is played occasionally. The bar hosts themed music evenings, evenings of rock-album music, concerts, party games, etc. One day of the week is dedicated to a selected rock star or band.

interests.title: Sir William's pub
interests.text: Located in the Art Nouveau part of Ljubljana city centre, offers more than 120 beer varieties and a large choice of teas and wines. It is vibrant in the evenings, particularly when it hosts themed events and beer tastings, while during the day it has a more coffee house-like atmosphere, offering its guests an opportunity to browse through a newspaper or read a book in peace. The staff speak several languages. Free wireless internet access is available.

interests.title: Metelkova mesto
interests.text: It's a squat with 6 clubs, each with its own style of music and program. Gala Hala is the biggest and the music is very diverse - from funk nights to hip-hop and indie to rap and strange fun music. It has also concerts and an outside garden where they also grill in the summer. Gromka is a smaller one usually admission free and the music also differs from night to night. Usually it's rock/alternative hits or rockabilly or fun 90s music. It has also concerts. Menza pri Koritu is the most alternative and it has mainly alternative, metal, punk concerts and after parties. Channel Zero is more about electronics (also concerts) - d'n'b, dub, electro, house. Jalla Jalla is the smallest one with sometimes obscure, experimental music. Tiffany is a gay club with usually electronic parties. In Metelkova everybody can find a place for himself (usually the most is happening on weekends) and you can just walk from one to another club and see what is going on (if there is no admission).

interests.title: K4
interests.text: National and international DJs play electronic music.

interests.title: Top
interests.text: Located on the top floor/roof of a downtown department store, this trendy nightclub offers fantastic views of downtown Ljubljana. Music varies depending on the day of the week.

destinations.name: Zagreb
destinations.insight: Gateway to the Adriatic. Underrated standalone, exceptional as a hinge.

interests.title: Zagreb Cathedral
interests.text: The Zagreb Cathedral with its twin spires is the most well known landmark of the city. The original cathedral dates back to 1217 but the present structure dates back to 1906. The chief architect was Hermann Bollé.

interests.title: St. Mark's Square
interests.text: Located at the centre of St. Mark's Square, St. Mark's Church is known for its colourful roof tiles depicting the coat of arms of Croatia and Zagreb. St. Mark's Church was constructed in 1499.

interests.title: St. Catherine's Church
interests.text: Baroque style church

interests.title: Old Town Gate
interests.text: now a shrine to virgin Mary - the '''"Kamenita vrata"''' where you can light a candle and, as the locals believe, your wish will be granted. Please be silent, even if just passing through the Gate. The portrait of Mary is said to be sacred, because it is the only thing that survived the large fire. Near the Old Town Gate is the sculpture of St. George having slain the dragon. The other statue, him fighting the dragon, is located on the Marshal Tito's Square near the Croatian National Theatre (roughly 1 km away).

interests.title: Ribnjak Park
interests.text: the Ribnjak is an English-style park complete with exotic plants, sculptures, and a children's playground. The park was originally the site of Bishop's fish pond.

interests.title: Museum of Broken Relationships (Muzej prekinutih veza)
interests.text: What remains after a break-up? Museum's permanent display is an opportunity to experience a unique emotional journey through dozens of love leftovers collected from all around the world. Museum is also the winner of Kenneth Hudson Award 2011 for the most innovative museum in Europe.

interests.title: Klovićevi Dvori Gallery
interests.text: This gallery in the old Jesuit monastery with changing exhibitions introduces Croatian and foreign cultural and artistic heritage across all eras. In most exhibitions, photography without flash is permitted.

interests.title: Strossmayer Square
interests.text: Named after Josip Juraj Strossmayer, politician and Roman Catholic Bishop, the Strossmayer Square occupies the central position of the eastern wing of the Lenuci Horseshoe.

interests.title: Zrinjevac Square
interests.text: the park of the Academy (connecting the Zrinjevac and Tomislav parks).

interests.title: King Tomislav Square
interests.text: King Tomislav Square is the square overlooking the main railway station (Glavni Kolodvor ) of Zagreb. Decorated with floor beds and fountains and lined with trees it is dominated by a horse mounted statue of King Tomislav.

interests.title: Mažuranić Square
interests.text: The central square of the western wing of Lenuci Horseshoe. It is named after Croatian poet Ivan Mažuranić

interests.title: Marulić Square
interests.text: Marulić Square is the South Western square of Lenuci Horseshoe. It houses the Croatia State Archive. It also has a statue of Marko Marulić.

interests.title: Square of Maršal Tito
interests.text: One of the largest and greenest squares of Zagreb. It is located on the North Western corner of Lenuci Horseshoe. It has the Croatian National Theatre in its centre.

interests.title: Muzej Mimara (Mimara Museum)
interests.text: Houses some 3,700 works of art donated by Ante Topić Mimara. The collection comprises more than 1,500 exhibits dating from the prehistoric period up to the 20th century. Some of the most famous exhibits include works by Lorenzetti, Raffaello, Giorgione, Veronese, Caravaggio, Canaletto, 60 paintings by the Dutch masters Rembrandt, Van Goyen, Ruisdael, 50 works by the Flemish masters Van der Weyden, Bosch, Rubens, Van Dyck, more than 30 by the Spanish masters Velasquez, Murillo, Goya, some 20 paintings by the German masters Holbein, Liebermann, Leibl, some 30 paintings by the English painters Gainsborough, Turner, Bonington and more than 120 paintings by the French masters Georges de la Tour, Boucher, Chardin, Delacroix, Corot, Manet, Renoir, Degas.

interests.title: Archaeological Museum
interests.text: Possesses over 400,000 objects, not all of them being exhibited. The holdings include evidence of Croatian presence in this area as well as rare samples which have made the museum known to the whole world. The most famous are the Egyptian collection, the Zagreb mummy and bandages with the oldest Etruscan inscription in the world (Liber Linteus Zagrabiensis), as well as the numismatic collection. A part of the museum is set aside for the collection of stone monuments dating back predominantly to the Roman period.

interests.title: Art Pavilion in Zagreb
interests.text: Designed by the Hungarian architects Korb and Giergl for the Millennium Exhibition 1896 in Budepest, its iron frame was transported to Zagreb after the exhibition, where the permanent pavillion was built by the famous Viennese designers of theatres in Central Europe Hellmer and Fellmer. It is a neo-classical exhibition complex and one of the landmarks of the city center. The exhibitions are also held in the impressive Meštrović pavilion on Trg žrtava fašizma. Current exhibition: Auguste Rodin - Rodin in Meštrović's Zagreb (until 20-Sep-2015), showing 55 large and small sculptures, 2 paintings and 10 scetches by Rodin and 10 early photographs of Rodin's work by then-famous photographers. Photography is not permitted by order of the Rodin museum in Paris despite the fact that Rodin's work is now in the public domain.

interests.title: Croatian National Theatre
interests.text: Croatian National Theatre, Zagreb is located at the centre of Marshal Tito Square. It is built in 1895. There are also Croatian National Theatres in Split, Rijeka, Osijek, Varaždin and Zadar.

interests.title: Moderna Galerija (Modern Gallery)
interests.text: Comprises all relevant fine artists of the 19th and 20th centuries.

interests.title: People and Art House Lauba (Kuća za ljude i umjetnost Lauba)
interests.text: Presents works from the Filip Trade Collection, a large private collection of modern and contemporary Croatian art and current artistic production.

interests.title: Technical Museum
interests.text: Has a lot of technical inventions, fire engines, a cabinet of Nikola Tesla, a planetarium and an underground mine.

interests.title: The Grounded Sun
interests.text: Prizemljeno sunce by Ivan Kožarić is a 1971 sculpture, a 2 meter wide bronze sphere, which represents the scale model of the Sun, with 9 planets of the solar system in their scaled size compared to this Sun. Mercury is in Margaretska street, a couple of hundred meters to the west, turn right after reaching Cvjetni trg, you should see it on the wall to the right after passing a dozen or so meters. Venus is on the pole of the building with Hypo bank on the Ban Jelačić Square. Earth is in Varšavska street to the left. For other models, click the link of the sculpture above.

interests.title: Maksimir Park & Zagreb Zoo
interests.text: The biggest park in Zagreb. Great place to chill out, drink coffee, eat lunch (picnic or at the park's restaurant) or take nice long walks through the forest or by the small lakes. Good for joggers, cyclists, also child and dog friendly. Offers lake rowing. The 85-year old zoo has red pandas.

interests.title: Mirogoj Cemetery
interests.text: Mirogoj is the central cemetery of Zagreb. It was opened in 1876 north of the city center. The mortuary, the impressive and picturesque arcades with the church of Christ the King (architect H. Bollé, 1883-1914) make it one of the most impressive cemeteries in the world. Here stand the tombs of notable personalities (including that of basketball hero Drazen Petrovic) who are relevant to Croatian history. To arrive from the central square, go to the nearby Cathedral and take bus number 106 (Kaptol - Mirogoj - Krematorij). The ride takes approximately 10 minutes. Note: the Croatian Day of the Dead is on November 1st. You may find the sight of hundreds of thousands of candles after nightfall impressive and worth seeing, but you may take note that on that day and a few preceding and following days the cemetery will be very busy and quite different from usual peaceful appearance; on those days buses to the cemetery are very frequent but crowded.

interests.title: Medvednica and Medvedgrad
interests.text: The imposing mountain overlooking Zagreb (Medvednica) contains a preserved fortress (Medvedgrad) and fascinating views of the city. Being respectful in the fort is a good idea, since it is the site of the Altar of the Homeland memorial, dedicated to Croatian soldiers who died in the Homeland war. There are several historical festivals during the year, including knights tournaments. (updated Jun 2015)

interests.title: Jarun
interests.text: Jarun Lake is a recreational area based around a large lake where locals go to relax, exercise - and party! The centerpiece of the area is the actual lake itself, which is used for windsurfing and has a large rowing section with spectator stands. There are also shingle beaches surrounding the water, alongside which are several bars that get very busy during the weekends but are also nice for a sunny lunchtime snack. All the bars there are considered 'fancy' places and usually play commercial music.

interests.title: Bundek
interests.text: hosts horticultural shows, firework festivals, workshops, concerts and music festivals (mostly during the summer).

interests.title: Museum of Contemporary Art
interests.text: Follows and presents contemporary trends in fine arts.

interests.title: Croatian Museum of Naïve Art
interests.text: Houses more than one thousand works belonging to Croatian naïve art.

interests.title: Strossmayerovo šetalište
interests.text: (eng. Strossmayer's walkway) In the summer, it is an outdoor scene for painters, musicians and other artists offering food, drinks, performances and concerts. Popular meeting place for all ages, with a great view of the Lower Town.

interests.title: Snow Queen Trophy
interests.text: Ladies Night Slalom : Zagreb is the only capital in the world that hosts a Ski World Cup race. The slalom event - known as the "Snow Queen Trophy" (Snježna kraljica) - usually takes place in January. It is the most expensive race on the women's world cup tour and an exceptionally popular sporting event in Croatia that attracts a crowd of around 25 thousand people. As of 2007 best skiers in men's competition are also expected to compete on Mount Sljeme that overlooks the city. If you fail to secure your ticket, you can watch the whole event on a giant screen in the Ban Jelačić Square.

interests.title: CineStar Zagreb
interests.text: A multiplex cinema (13 screens) in the vicinity of the Sheraton Hotel. The website is in Croatian, but can be understood using a Croatian-English online dictionary.

interests.title: CineStar Novi Zagreb
interests.text: A multiplex cinema (9 screens) in the vicinity of the Museum of Contemporary Art.

interests.title: Cineplexx Centar Kaptol
interests.text: A multiplex cinema (5 screens) within the Kaptol Shopping Center situated to the north of the Cathedral.

interests.title: Cineplexx City Center one East
interests.text: A multiplex cinema (7 screens) located on the east side of Zagreb.

interests.title: Kino Tuškanac
interests.text: Showing much more than just European filmography, alternative and older films, sometimes for free, usually costs about 10 HRK (€1.50)

interests.title: American Doughnut
interests.text: A sandwich bar that also serves salads and deserts.

interests.title: Baschiera 2
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: Bonita
interests.text: Popular sandwich bar that also serves pancakes and hot wine in the winter.

interests.title: Colonia
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: Dvojka
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: Gigi
interests.text: Be sure to visit this pizzeria if you're going to the Jarun lake.

interests.title: Karijola
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: Koykan World Food
interests.text: Italian piadinas, Japanese teriyaki sticks, oriental wraps, Bubble tea from Taiwan and Japanese mochi cakes.

interests.title: Mamma Mia
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: MOCA
interests.text: Newly open at the main square serves great and cheap fried, deep fried and baked potatoes with a large variety of dips for takeout.

interests.title: Nocturno
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: Nova
interests.text: Vegetarian.

interests.title: Oliva
interests.text: Italian restaurant (pizza, pasta)

interests.title: Pizza Cut Duck
interests.text: A pizza-slice bar that also serves fresh tortillas with veggie and/or meat filling.

interests.title: Pyr
interests.text: Vegetarian.

interests.title: Robin Food Catering
interests.text: Vegetarian.

interests.title: Sandwich Bar Pingvin
interests.text: Classic sandwich bar with a wide menu and reasonably good mix of languages. One of the most popular dishes is the toplo/hladno (hot/cold), a grilled chicken and vegetables sandwich.

interests.title: Šestica
interests.text: Pizza.

interests.title: Stara Sava
interests.text: Arguably the best pizzeria in Zagreb.

interests.title: VegeHop
interests.text: Vegetarian.

interests.title: Žirafa
interests.text: Cheap Mexican food and drinks. Nice summer terrace.

interests.title: Royal India
interests.text: Opened in Aug. 2013 Royal India serves authentic Indian Dishes.

interests.title: Asia
interests.text: Chinese restaurant

interests.title: Caramba
interests.text: Mexican food.

interests.title: Ivica i Marica
interests.text: Wonderful food made only from natural ingredients, many sourced locally.

interests.title: Mali Medo
interests.text: Another traditional Croatian restaurant. Lively atmosphere, youthful clientele, and all very affordable. Excellent ćevapčići and gulaš, not to mention superb beer.

interests.title: Manzoku
interests.text: An excellent and so far only Japanese take-out in Zagreb. Tasty but rather expensive.

interests.title: Mex Cantina
interests.text: Mexican food. Good service, great food, on Mondays they offer live music by the best Croatian mariachi band, Los Caballeros starting at 9PM.

interests.title: Pivnica Medvedgrad
interests.text: Great food and beer for reasonable prices. Make sure to try 'čvarkuša' (4 HRK).

interests.title: Restaurant Kvatric
interests.text: Croatian restaurant with very nice food for a reasonable price. Has a nice terrance in the back garden. Close to tramstop 'Kvaternikov Trg'.

interests.title: Boban
interests.text: Italian food.

interests.title: Feniks
interests.text: Mexican food.

interests.title: Le Bistro
interests.text: A French restaurant within the Regent Esplanade Hotel..

interests.title: Panino
interests.text: Nice little restaurant with great service and wonderful food. If you are into French and Istrian style food you will probably love this place.

interests.title: Restaurant Suhina
interests.text: An old family-run restaurant on the old Samobor road that offers a variety of roasted food.

interests.title: Srčeko
interests.text: A very romantic little restaurant.

interests.title: Takenoko
interests.text: Zagreb may not be synonymous with sushi restaurants, but this is one is superb. It is not cheap, but sushi they make is incredible.

interests.title: Velika Klet Obitelji Bunčić
interests.text: A very popular place that offers Croatian cuisine, live music on weekends, a stable, fish pond and basketball courtyard.

interests.title: 100% juice bar
interests.text: Pricey but a good place to get a healthy snack or fruit smoothie.

interests.title: Ivica and Marica
interests.text: Offers traditional and diet, diabetic and organic cakes, ice creams and such.

interests.title: SladoMazo
interests.text: A pancake restaurant with a wide range of crazy both sweet and satly filling ideas.

interests.title: The Cookie Factory
interests.text: Homebaked American Sweets. Cookies, brownies and bars, krispy treats, muffins, cupcakes, banana breads, scones, cobblers, biscotti, top notch coffee, smoothies, cakes and pies.

interests.title: Torte i To
interests.text: A great little caffe offering the best cheese cake in town, and other great cakes

interests.title: Vincek
interests.text: The local, best known ice cream and cake palace in town center. Offers diet foods.

interests.title: Konoba Didov San
interests.text: The best atmosphere restaurant in Zagreb with excellent food, unique menu, large portions and great location!!

interests.title: Nishta Restaurant
interests.text: 100 % Vegetarian food, also has a branch in Dubrovnik

interests.title: Booksa
interests.text: A cafe/bookshop/library famous with local young intelectuals.

interests.title: Harlekin
interests.text: Cozy atmosphere, excellent English, free Wi-Fi, delicious drinks, very reasonable prices, great coffee, DJ or live music on weekends.

interests.title: Cica
interests.text: Bar mostly known for its great and varied choice of the local schnappses (rakija). Interesting always changing, gallery-like interior. The whole bar is small but offers outdoor seating. Very popular and crowded, brimming with young business and student clientele.

interests.title: Funk club
interests.text: Cafe / bar that offers various music night programmes in the basement (mostly alternative, dj-s, world music) and spacial backpacker evenings. Very good atmosphere, lots of character, cheap and tasty Rakija shoots and excellent electronic music: funky, electro, jazz, d'n'b, house, dubstep.

interests.title: Golf
interests.text: Popular golf-themed cafe/bar.

interests.title: KIC
interests.text: Offers free internet and cheap international call center. Hosts a cinema with various alternative movies.

interests.title: Kino Grič
interests.text: Newly re-decorated old cinema. Hosts movies, festivals, music nights and Zagreb's hipster crowd.

interests.title: Melin
interests.text: A cheap bar brimming with young people and students. Offers indoors, a roofed terraced leveled seating and an open terrace facing Tkalčićeva street. Known for crowds of people often drinking even around the bar, in the park and playground in front of it.

interests.title: Oliver Twist
interests.text: A choice of good Irish beer with a great atmosphere. Big summer terrace in front.

interests.title: Bar Plan B
interests.text: A cafe/wine bar/sports bar. Attracts a mix of locals and tourists. You can expect music from every genre, and decade, from Jazz to Alternative/Indie.

interests.title: Portal
interests.text: Next to Melin, offers same service, the Portal and Melin crowds often mix at the outdoors terraces.

interests.title: Purgeraj
interests.text: A bar/club located in the Ribnjak park in the city center. Offers mostly rock, blues and alternative music. Daily happy hour when you get two drinks for the price of one. Sometimes hosts concerts. Popular with student crowd esp on Thursdays when it hosts the newly popular "take me out" evening offering a mix of indie and alternative music. In the summer, outside Purgeraj, in the Ribnjak park there are live concerts and various workshops offering summer fun under the name 'Park In Zagreb'.

interests.title: Tolkien's House
interests.text: A small fantasy cafe/bar for Tolkien lovers. Offers a variety of beers, cider and hot drinks.

interests.title: Aquarius
interests.text: Saturday night is drum'n'bass & dancehall music, Friday is hip-hop/r'n'b night. Good concerts.

interests.title: BP Club
interests.text: Jazz and blues lovers should check it out.

interests.title: Jabuka
interests.text: Cult club for alternative, punk music. Cheap drinks opened on weekends, often concerts of alternative bands. Mostly visited on weekends, especially Fridays. Admission fee: 25 Kn.

interests.title: KSET
interests.text: A popular student club. Offers various indie and alternative programmes every night of the week. Indoors smoking prohibited. Serves only beer and non alcoholic drinks.

interests.title: Medika
interests.text: A local alternative club/legalized squat usually loaded with people during weekends, playing various music genres until the early morning. Best Croatian beers for 10 HRK.

interests.title: Močvara
interests.text: A local alternative club (rock, punk, metal, indie)with various concerts and theme nights/programmes. Sometimes hosts festivals, young indie talent concerts, movie nights, exhibitions etc.

interests.title: SAX! - Klub hrvatskih glazbenika
interests.text: A great place to enjoy live music of a wide array of styles including blues, jazz, rock and pop.

interests.title: Sirup
interests.text: Interesting interior and atmosphere. Good electronic music. A bit expensive entrance fees, but worth seeing.

interests.title: The Best
interests.text: Largest club in Zagreb. Mainly dance style music.

interests.title: The Jazz Club
interests.text: Jazz, blues, and funk bands, often from other countries.

destinations.name: Athens
destinations.insight: Far south, mostly by air. Pay the carbon, take the millennia.

destinations.name: Istanbul
destinations.insight: Edge of the continent. Dense, layered, demanding — in the best sense.

destinations.name: Sofia
destinations.insight: Underexplored. Affordable once you arrive — costly to get there cleanly.

interests.title: Sofia Public Mineral Baths
interests.text: It is an old building of interest. It was renovated and is now a museum of the history of Sofia.

interests.title: National Museum of Military History
interests.text: ticket price for adults 8 leva (on March-15-2012)

interests.title: Institute of Ethnology and Folklore Studies with Ethnographic Museum
interests.text: Has a permanent collection of traditional Bulgarian costumes as well as a changing exhibition.

interests.title: Earth and Man National Museum
interests.text: Has the biggest collection of gigantic minerals in the World.

interests.title: National Museum of History
interests.text: The National Museum of History (Национален исторически музей) in the Rezidentsia Boyana in Sofia contains more than 650,000 exhibits and is one of the largest history museums on the Balkans, so be prepared to spend several hours. The aim of the museum is to provide a comprehensive view on Bulgarian history from the prehistory to present, in as broad of a European context as possible. One main attraction are golden or silver mugs and jewelery that date from Thracian (around 4th century BC) and roman periods. About half of the exhibits have English captions.

interests.title: National Museum of Natural History
interests.text: Has four floors of everything from rocks and minerals to insects and stuffed bison. It is a nice way to spend a rainy afternoon.

interests.title: National Art Gallery
interests.text: Has old Bulgarian art, icons, etc.

interests.title: St. Aleksander Nevski Cathedral
interests.text: One of the largest orthodox churches worldwide and the largest in the Balkan Peninsula.

interests.title: Boyana Church
interests.text: This small 14th-century church and garden is on the UNESCO World Heritage List. The church contains some very well preserved murals. It is located at the foot of Vitosha mountain and is a good starting point for day trips in the mountain. Entrance is 10 Lev, or 2 Lev for students.

interests.title: Hagia Sophia Church
interests.text: It is located just across the square and to the right from the Aleksandar Nevski Cathedral. It was built in the 6th century over an even older church. It is a witness of the whole Bulgarian history and is a valued cultural monument. In the 14th century it gave its name to the city of Sofia. It was destroyed several times and during the Ottoman rule it was used as a mosque.

interests.title: Catholic cathedral St. Joseph
interests.text: Holy Masses: SAT 5,30pm & SUN 10,30am bulgarian; 12,00pm latin; 6PM bulgarian

interests.title: Sofia Synagogue
interests.text: One of the largest in Europe.

interests.title: Battenberg Mausoleum
interests.text: The mausoleum and final resting place of Prince Alexander I of Bulgaria (1857–1893), the first Head of State of modern Bulgaria.

interests.title: Monument to Vasil Levski
interests.text: It commemorates the hanging of Bulgarian national hero and major revolutionary figure Vasil Levski in the city on 18 February 1873.

interests.title: Monument to the Tsar Liberator
interests.text: Representing the power of freedom and across from it are the National Assembly and the Bulgarian Academy of Sciences.

interests.title: Monument to the Unknown Soldier
interests.text: Across from Aleksandar Nevski cathedral; on the side of Hagia Sofia's Church.

interests.title: Tsar Samuil Monument
interests.text: A statue of the bulgarian warlord and tsar Samuil, who ruled Bulgaria between 997 and 1014.

interests.title: Russian Monument
interests.text: An obelisk built by the Russians.

interests.title: Largo
interests.text: 3 buildings of Socialist Classicism architecture.

interests.title: Prince Alexander of Battenberg Square
interests.text: The largest square in Sofia. Includes the National Art Gallery.

interests.title: Petko R. Slaveykov Square
interests.text: A Famous Square, real Sofia center with a monument of writers father and son Slaveykov's. It`s a biggest open-air book market in the Balkans.There are round benches and street musicians act very often.

interests.title: Patriarh Evtimiy Square
interests.text: A popular meeting place.

interests.title: Borisova Gradina (Boris Garden)
interests.text: The oldest and best known park in the city. Includes the Ariana Lake.

interests.title: City Garden
interests.text: It's the oldest and most central public garden, in existence since 1872.

interests.title: Yuzhen Park (South Park)
interests.text: A nice park in the south-east part of the city, although a bit too crowded on weekends. Vibrant with open area cafes, entertainment for children and grown-ups, and a few ponds with ducks.

interests.title: Oborishte Park
interests.text: A park near the center of the city with places to eat and drink. Also known as "Zaimov Park".

interests.title: Orlov Most
interests.text: A bridge over the Perlovska River near the centre of Sofia

interests.title: Lavov Most
interests.text: A bridge over the Vladayska River near the central train station.

interests.title: National Palace of Culture
interests.text: The biggest congress center in the Balkans (a massive monolithic communist-style building). If asking for directions, ask for NDK (en-de-ka), as most Bulgarians refer to it by this acronym.

interests.title: Central Military Club
interests.text: It`s a multi-purpose monument of culture building in the city center. Also known under the name Kristal.

interests.title: Cafes and restaurants
interests.text: Sofia is full of trendy cafes with outdoor seating in the summer and good-quality restaurants.

interests.title: Sofia Opera and Ballet
interests.text: During summer, open air shows in the evenings are offered.

interests.title: Arena Cinema
interests.text: One of the biggest Bulgarian cinema multiplex. Cinemas in Sofia play all films except children's films in their original language with Bulgarian subtitles.

interests.title: Sofia Bike Tour
interests.text: Visit the greenest capital of Europe on a bike. Every day Free Bike Tour (12 April–November) at 11AM & 5PM. Escape from the city and join to the Bike Countryside Tour around Sofia. Start Point: National Theater "Ivan Vazov"

interests.title: Playgrounds
interests.text: If you're traveling with kids, they might love playgrounds. The "rich" one is in the park that holds Vasil Levski stadium (close to the corner with Orlov Most; there are actually two playgrounds close to this corner and you'd look for the one more inside the park).

interests.title: Eat with a local Bulgarian Host
interests.text: Experience a complete 4 course authentic Bulgarian food with a local chef at her private garden in Sofia city. Get a taste of local culture and authentic food.

interests.title: Mr.Pizza
interests.text: Popular in Sofia, get there early or reserve if you want a table in their courtyard.

interests.title: Baаlbek
interests.text: This is great Arab food. Downstairs is take-away kebabs and falafels, and upstairs a small eat-in. Excellent hummus and salads, etc., and cold beer.

interests.title: Trops House
interests.text: It`s a fast-food type of place, offering traditional Bulgarian meals and drinks. It can be found in several locations throughout the city center.

interests.title: Happy's Bar and Grill
interests.text: Happy's is the Bulgarian equivalent of an American diner, and the Sofia representative of a nationwide chain. There are English-language menus, and the staff (all microskirted young girls, who apparently have to be smiling when they serve you) all seem to have at least some English as well. Portions are generally huge and represent a sort of "watered down" version of their traditional Bulgarian equivalents, although more Western fare is also available.

interests.title: Pizza Victoria
interests.text: One of the best Bulgarian pizzas.

interests.title: GODZILA
interests.text: GODZILA's first restaurant opened in 1999 in the sea capital of Bulgaria - Varna . There you can try some fresh salads, juicy barbecue, delicious pizza and various other dishes. The menus are also written in English and the staff speaks English as well. There are traditional Bulgarian cuisine and Western cuisine as well.

interests.title: Pod Lipite
interests.text: Very good traditional food. Reservation is required.

interests.title: Motto
interests.text: This place is always fairly busy with a trendy crowd. The decor is modern and cool, and the people typically good looking. The service here is usually pretty nice, and many of the wait-staff speak English. The food is consistent and enjoyable.

interests.title: Carrera
interests.text: A fairly modern restaurant over in the Lozenetz area. The decor is well done, the service good, and the food very good. Prices are in line with the quality and style of the place. The wine selection is excellent. They also have a nice summer/winter garden area.

interests.title: Hadjidraganovite kashti
interests.text: Amazing restaurant just off one of Sofia's main streets. The decor is old-style Bulgarian and the food is traditional Bulgarian, plentiful and very, very tasty. Entertainment includes a Bulgarian walking band and guests are primarily locals. Staff is very friendly and most speak English. Reservations are necessary, even on weeknights.

interests.title: Mehana Karajata
